results.title: How Do You Deal With "My Computer Is Slow, I Need a New One" Complaints? - IT & Tech Careers - Spiceworks Community
results.content:
# How Do You Deal With "My Computer Is Slow, I Need a New One" Complaints?

This problem user is a VP (why, I don’t have a clue). She specializes in throwing people under the proverbial bus. A couple of months ago, we gave her a brand new Dell Laptop with a docking station. The laptop is faster than all IT desktops–Intel Core 2 with 4 GB of RAM.

This user has had issues with her computer for months. Each time she exaggerates the issue. Today she calls me bitching about her computer being “slow”. “Slow” is a vague and broad description of a problem. All I can do is hypothesize. She doesn’t like this. So I have her log in while I shadow her remotely. The computer was fine. Supposedly the issue only happens when she’s using Outlook. The computer freezes up and she has to hard boot it. [...] ### Related topics

| The Dreaded Ticket "My computer is being slow"  IT & Tech Careers discussion ,  best-practices | 178 | 1246 | January 5, 2017 |
| The one "request" or email/call I absolutely despise...  Water Cooler discussion ,  water-cooler | 36 | 262 | January 5, 2022 |
| How do you deal with impatient users?  IT & Tech Careers discussion ,  best-practices | 64 | 449 | July 9, 2015 |
| "My computer is slow" - What do you do?  Software & Applications discussion ,  general-windows ,  windows-7 | 74 | 578 | June 18, 2014 | [...] 2.When you have been round to their desk and still not seen any issues, after doing some useless stuff like looking in MSConfig, Ping testing the the servers, flushing DNS cache, checking installed programs etc,. What you need to say is “The fault doesn’t seem to be occurring at the moment, unfortunately this makes it impossible to diagnose and fix. What we need to do is have me either remote in or come round the next time it happens. So give me a call the next time it runs slow and I can see the problem first hand.” This again says you do care that there is a fault and that you can fix it but not until you see it.

results.title: COMPLAINTS ABOUT WINDOWS 10 AND A REQUEST FOR URGENT ASSISTANCE - Microsoft Q&A
results.content:
If you want to complain to Microsoft use the Feedback Hub app in Start Menu where developers are tasked to process consumer feedback. But Microsoft will not even see it here because this is a tech forums where we are mostly volunteers trying to help solve your problems.

I would uninstall anything from Avast now in Settings > Apps > Apps & Features, then run Avast Clear to get it all out:

Here are the latest ways to control Updates:

If you've had enough and need a break you can set a Metered Connection which is the only stable method to turn them off:

You can reset the theme by setting a new Theme from Settings > Personalization > Themes or from the many spectacular free Themes you can download from the Store to that Themes panel to choose from: [...] As you may have picked up from the foregoing message, I am VERY UPSET.  Please advise whether I can speak to a REAL PERSON who would be able to access my computer remotely and help me with these issues.

Thank you.

(b)

results.title: 5 Signs Your Computer is Crying Out for Repair – Abrisuite
results.content:
1. It’s running slow.  Most people assume their computer is running slow because it’s getting older, but it could actually be a variety of reasons. A program behaving badly, a virus, overheating or even a failing hard drive can all cause a massive slow down. You might only notice it when booting up or starting a program, or the problem may have taken hold to the extent that even moving your mouse becomes torture. Sometimes the slow speed is simply due to some newer software that your hardware can’t keep up with. [...] 3. Blue Screens of Death are everywhere. A classic Windows error, this is quite literally a blue screen that covers your view. The system will still be running, but something has gone wrong. You’ll be shown some text and an error code, often with Windows suggesting a restart. If a restart fixes your problem, perhaps something didn’t load properly at bootup and your computer had a whoopsie. It’s rare, but it happens. If you’re getting blue screens all the time though, that’s a sign a hardware or software problem needs to be resolved. Your computer will continue to give blue screen errors more and more frequently, so it’s best to take action as soon as you know something’s wrong. [...] All • Managed IT

Summary

Share

It’s pretty obvious when your computer is already broken, but how do you know when it’s about to break? Even before it falls into a heap and refuses to turn on, or flashes big messages about how your files are now encrypted, you’ll be given multiple hints that something is wrong. Here are the common signs your computer needs repair, sooner rather than later.

We offer same-day service nationwide, so in most cases you can get connected with a technician the very day you call. Yes! Our services are nationwide. We deliver secure remote computer repair to many customers across the U.S.
